Replication of ‘Sick Body, Vigilant Mind’

Josh Tybur, Ben Jones, Lisa DeBruine

  • Load and Process Data from Inquisit
    • Simulate Raters
    • Recode
    • Simulate Trials
    • Exclusions
    • Join Data
  • Descriptives
    • Demographics
    • Included trials
    • RTs by trial
    • Illness Variables
    • Face Type by Recent Cold

    • Reaction Time DV
      • Analysis 1A: Categorical illness recency
      • Analysis 1B: Recent illness
      • Analysis 1C: Non-Recent illness
      • Analysis 1D: Equivalence Tests

      • Analysis 2A: Perceived Infectability
      • Analysis 2B: Germ Aversion
      • Analysis 2C: TDDS
      • Analysis 2D: Curtis Images
library(TOSTER) # for equivalence tests
library(afex) # for SPSS-style ANOVAs; also loads lme4 and lmerTest for LMEMs
library(tidyverse) # for data wrangling

Load and Process Data from Inquisit

dat.raw <- read_tsv("Dot_Probe_raw.dat")
## Parsed with column specification:
## cols(
##   subject = col_double(),
##   blockcode = col_character(),
##   trialcode = col_character(),
##   trialnum = col_double(),
##   latency = col_double(),
##   response = col_character(),
##   stimulusitem1 = col_character(),
##   stimulusitem2 = col_character(),
##   stimulusitem3 = col_character(),
##   stimulusitem4 = col_character(),
##   stimulusitem5 = col_character()
## )
trials <- dat.raw %>%
  mutate(
    stim_id = str_replace(stimulusitem3, ".bmp", ""),
    target = str_replace(stimulusitem5, ".bmp", "")
  ) %>%
  select(-(stimulusitem1:stimulusitem5)) %>%
  filter(blockcode != "practice") %>%
  separate(trialcode, c("real", "position", "trial_type", "block")) %>%
  separate(stim_id, c("w", "face_sex", "face_type", "face_n"), sep = c(1, 2,3), remove = F) %>%
  select(-real, -w, -face_n) %>%
  mutate( # circle = 18, square = 23
    correct = case_when(
      target == "circle" & response == 18 ~ 1,
      target == "square" & response == 23 ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ 0
    ),
    face_type = recode(face_type, "d" = "disfigured", "n" = "normal")
  ) %>%
  rename(rt = latency, user_id = subject)

Simulate Raters

  • recency (4 items) Illness recency
  • PVDpi (7 items) Perceived infectability subscale of PVD
  • PVDga (7 items) Germ aversion subscale of PVD
  • pd (7 items) Pathogen disgust subscale of TDDS
  • img (7 items) Pathogen images from Curtis
n <- 300 #original = 96

raters <- tibble(
  user_id = 1:n,
  sex = c(rep("male", ceiling(36/96*n)), 
          rep("female", floor(60/96*n))
          ),
  age = sample(18:30, n, replace = T),
  # distrubute so ~28% are recently ill
  recent_cold = sample(1:7, n, replace = T, 
                       prob = c(.05, .10, .13, .44, .13, .10, .05)),
  recency_1 = sample(1:7, n, replace = T),
  recency_2 = sample(1:7, n, replace = T),
  recency_3 = sample(1:7, n, replace = T),
  recency_4 = sample(1:7, n, replace = T),
  PVDpi_1 = sample(0:6, n, replace = T),
  PVDpi_2 = sample(0:6, n, replace = T),
  PVDpi_3 = sample(0:6, n, replace = T),
  PVDpi_4 = sample(0:6, n, replace = T),
  PVDpi_5 = sample(0:6, n, replace = T),
  PVDpi_6 = sample(0:6, n, replace = T),
  PVDpi_7 = sample(0:6, n, replace = T),
  PVDga_1 = sample(0:6, n, replace = T),
  PVDga_2 = sample(0:6, n, replace = T),
  PVDga_3 = sample(0:6, n, replace = T),
  PVDga_4 = sample(0:6, n, replace = T),
  PVDga_5 = sample(0:6, n, replace = T),
  PVDga_6 = sample(0:6, n, replace = T),
  PVDga_7 = sample(0:6, n, replace = T),
  PVDga_8 = sample(0:6, n, replace = T),
  pd_1 = sample(0:6, n, replace = T),
  pd_2 = sample(0:6, n, replace = T),
  pd_3 = sample(0:6, n, replace = T),
  pd_4 = sample(0:6, n, replace = T),
  pd_5 = sample(0:6, n, replace = T),
  pd_6 = sample(0:6, n, replace = T),
  pd_7 = sample(0:6, n, replace = T),
  img_1 = sample(1:7, n, replace = T),
  img_2 = sample(1:7, n, replace = T),
  img_3 = sample(1:7, n, replace = T),
  img_4 = sample(1:7, n, replace = T),
  img_5 = sample(1:7, n, replace = T),
  img_6 = sample(1:7, n, replace = T),
  img_7 = sample(1:7, n, replace = T),
  img_8 = sample(1:7, n, replace = T)
)

Recode

raters_coded <- raters %>%
  mutate(
    recent_cold = case_when(
      recent_cold %in% 1:3 ~ "recent",
      recent_cold %in% 4:7 ~ "not recent"
    )
  ) %>%
  gather(var, score, recency_1:img_8) %>%
  separate(var, c("var", "var_n")) %>%
  group_by(user_id, sex, age, recent_cold, var) %>%
  summarise(score = mean(score)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  spread(var, score)

Simulate Trials

trials <- expand.grid(
  user_id = 1:n,
  trial = 1:80
) %>%
  mutate(
    trial_type = ifelse(trial <= 80*.4, "same", "diff"),
    face_type = as.factor(ifelse(trial%%2 == 0, "disfigured", "normal")),
    correct = sample(0:1, nrow(.), replace = T, prob = c(.1, .9)),
    rt = rnorm(nrow(.), 620, 160) # approx mean & sd from SBVM
  )

Exclusions

“Trials with incorrect categorization responses and trials with latencies greater than 3 standard deviations above or below a participant’s mean latency were excluded. Two participants had high error rates (> 3 SD above the mean) and were excluded.” (Miller & Maner)

trials_inc <- trials %>%
  filter(correct == 1, trial_type == "diff") %>%
  group_by(user_id) %>%
  mutate(mean_rt = mean(rt),
         sd_rt = sd(rt),
         n_correct = n()) %>%
  filter(rt <= mean_rt + 3*sd_rt, rt >= mean_rt - 3*sd_rt) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by() %>%
  filter(n_correct >= mean(n_correct) - 3*sd(n_correct)) %>%
  ungroup()

Join Data

# centre and scale continuous scores from only included raters
raters_inc <- raters_coded %>%
  semi_join(trials_inc, by = "user_id") %>%
  mutate(
    recent_cold = as.factor(recent_cold),
    recency.z = (recency - mean(recency)) / sd(recency),
    PVDga.z = (PVDga - mean(PVDga)) / sd(PVDga),
    PVDpi.z = (PVDpi - mean(PVDpi)) / sd(PVDpi)
  )

data_agg <- trials_inc %>%
  group_by(user_id, face_type) %>%
  summarise(rt = mean(rt)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  left_join(raters_inc, by = "user_id")

Descriptives

Demographics

raters_coded %>%
  group_by(sex) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_age = mean(age, na.rm = T),
    sd_age = sd(age, na.rm = T)
  ) %>%
  bind_rows(raters_coded %>%
    group_by() %>%
    summarise(
      n = n(),
      mean_age = mean(age, na.rm = T),
      sd_age = sd(age, na.rm = T)
    ) %>%
    mutate(sex = "TOTAL")
  ) %>%
  mutate_if(is.numeric, round, 2)

Included trials

trials_inc %>%
  group_by(user_id, face_type) %>%
  summarise(included_trials = n()) %>%
  group_by(face_type) %>%
  summarise(n = n(),
            mean = mean(included_trials),
            min = min(included_trials),
            max = max(included_trials)
  ) %>%
  mutate_if(is.numeric, round, 2)

RTs by trial

trials_inc %>%
  ggplot(aes(rt, color = face_type, group = as.factor(trial))) +
  geom_density() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#CC0000", "#006699"), name = "Face Type") +
  theme_minimal()

Illness Variables

raters_coded %>%
  gather(variable, score, recency, PVDga, PVDpi) %>%
  ggplot(aes(score, color = variable)) +
  geom_density()

Face Type by Recent Cold

data_agg %>%
  group_by(face_type, recent_cold) %>%
  summarise(n = n(),
            mean = mean(rt),
            sd = sd(rt)
  ) %>%
  mutate_if(is.numeric, round, 3)
ggplot(data_agg, aes(recent_cold, rt, color = face_type)) +
  geom_violin(aes(fill = face_type), alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.2, outlier.size = 0,
               position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
               show.legend = F) +
  labs(x = "Recency of Cold",
       y = "Mean RT on Attentional Shift Trials") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#CC0000", "#006699"), name = "Face Type") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#CC0000", "#006699"), guide = F) +
  theme_minimal()

Analyses

Reaction Time DV

Analysis 1A: Categorical illness recency

cat_ANOVA <- aov_4(rt ~ recent_cold * face_type + 
                     (1 + face_type | user_id), 
              data = data_agg, factorize = FALSE)
## Contrasts set to contr.sum for the following variables: recent_cold
anova(cat_ANOVA, es = "pes", MSE = FALSE)

Analysis 1B: Recent illness

recent_ill <- filter(data_agg, recent_cold == "recent")
t.test(recent_ill$rt~recent_ill$face_type)
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  recent_ill$rt by recent_ill$face_type
## t = -1.4699, df = 162, p-value = 0.1435
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -17.472002   2.560578
## sample estimates:
## mean in group disfigured     mean in group normal 
##                 617.8869                 625.3426

Analysis 1C: Non-Recent illness

not_recent_ill <- filter(data_agg, recent_cold == "not recent")
t.test(not_recent_ill$rt~not_recent_ill$face_type)
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  not_recent_ill$rt by not_recent_ill$face_type
## t = 0.027908, df = 430.23, p-value = 0.9777
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -5.950568  6.121985
## sample estimates:
## mean in group disfigured     mean in group normal 
##                 618.9519                 618.8662

Analysis 1D: Equivalence Tests

stats_cat <- data_agg %>%
  spread(face_type, rt, convert = T) %>%
  mutate(dv = disfigured - normal) %>%
  group_by(recent_cold) %>%
  summarise(n = n(),
            m = mean(dv),
            sd = sd(dv)
  )

n1 <- stats_cat %>% filter(recent_cold == "not recent") %>% pull(n)
n2 <- stats_cat %>% filter(recent_cold == "recent") %>% pull(n)
m1 <- stats_cat %>% filter(recent_cold == "not recent") %>% pull(m)
m2 <- stats_cat %>% filter(recent_cold == "recent") %>% pull(m)
sd1 <- stats_cat %>% filter(recent_cold == "not recent") %>% pull(sd)
sd2 <- stats_cat %>% filter(recent_cold == "recent") %>% pull(sd)
low_eqbound_dz <- -.35
high_eqbound_dz <- .35

TOSTtwo(m1, m2, sd1, sd2, n1, n2, low_eqbound_dz, high_eqbound_dz)
## TOST results:
## t-value lower bound: 4.36    p-value lower bound: 0.00001
## t-value upper bound: -1.38   p-value upper bound: 0.084
## degrees of freedom : 188.45
## 
## Equivalence bounds (Cohen's d):
## low eqbound: -0.35 
## high eqbound: 0.35
## 
## Equivalence bounds (raw scores):
## low eqbound: -14.5558 
## high eqbound: 14.5558
## 
## TOST confidence interval:
## lower bound 90% CI: -0.84
## upper bound 90% CI:  15.923
## 
## NHST confidence interval:
## lower bound 95% CI: -2.461
## upper bound 95% CI:  17.543
## 
## Equivalence Test Result:
## The equivalence test was non-significant, t(188.45) = -1.383, p = 0.0841, given equivalence bounds of -14.556 and 14.556 (on a raw scale) and an alpha of 0.05.
## 
## 
## Null Hypothesis Test Result:
## The null hypothesis test was non-significant, t(188.45) = 1.487, p = 0.139, given an alpha of 0.05.
## 
## 
## Based on the equivalence test and the null-hypothesis test combined, we can conclude that the observed effect is statistically not different from zero and statistically not equivalent to zero.
##

Continuous illness recency

cont_ANOVA <- aov_4(rt ~ recency.z * face_type + 
                      (1 + face_type | user_id), 
              data = data_agg, factorize = FALSE)

anova(cont_ANOVA, es = "pes", MSE = FALSE)

Self-report Measures

Difference in PVD between recently ill and non-recently ill groups

Analysis 2A: Perceived Infectability

t.test(data_agg$PVDpi~data_agg$recent_cold)
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  data_agg$PVDpi by data_agg$recent_cold
## t = 0.3176, df = 246.79, p-value = 0.7511
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.1196430  0.1656456
## sample estimates:
## mean in group not recent     mean in group recent 
##                 3.017775                 2.994774

Analysis 2B: Germ Aversion

t.test(data_agg$PVDga~data_agg$recent_cold)
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  data_agg$PVDga by data_agg$recent_cold
## t = -0.97062, df = 316.54, p-value = 0.3325
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.17870699  0.06063348
## sample estimates:
## mean in group not recent     mean in group recent 
##                 3.029378                 3.088415

Analysis 2C: TDDS

t.test(data_agg$pd~data_agg$recent_cold)
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  data_agg$pd by data_agg$recent_cold
## t = 1.1886, df = 338.32, p-value = 0.2354
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.04715019  0.19114736
## sample estimates:
## mean in group not recent     mean in group recent 
##                 2.960500                 2.888502

Analysis 2D: Curtis Images

t.test(data_agg$img~data_agg$recent_cold)
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  data_agg$img by data_agg$recent_cold
## t = 3.0263, df = 290.53, p-value = 0.002697
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.07389393 0.34877663
## sample estimates:
## mean in group not recent     mean in group recent 
##                 4.077189                 3.865854
